chunks.text: HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-1242 of 2023 [Faisal Ahmed vs. Province of Sindh and Others] BEFORE: JUSTICE ADNAN-UL-KARIM MEMON JUSTICE RIAZAT ALI SAHAR Mr. Saleem Raza Soomro, advocate for petitioner Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Additional A.G. Sindh Date of hearing & decision: 24.11.2025 ORDER ADNAN-UL-KARIM MEMON J. - The petitioner, through the captioned petition, has sought the following relief(s):- a) Direct the respondents No.1 to 2 to appoint a teacher of GBPS village Haji Ghulam Mustafa Memon, Taluka & District Matiari, to save the future of poor students from ruin. b) Direct the respondents No. 1 & 2 to conduct an inquiry regarding the non-availability of school teachers in the whole District Matiari and to take immediate action against the respondents No. 3 & 4. 2. Learned counsel for the petitioner contends that the petitioner, a permanent resident of Village Ghulam Mustafa, UC Faquir Nooh Hothiyani, Taluka & District Matiari, states that the village has a population of about 2,500 and in the village there is Government Boys Primary School with only one teacher for 180 students who has also been transferred due to his promotion and now there is no teacher in the school. He submits that despite repeated requests by the villagers for an additional teacher, no action has been taken; hence the instant Petition. 3. Learned Additional A.G. has challenged the maintainability of the petition, stating that posting of teacher is an administrative matters made based on need and not on the request of an individual; therefore the petition is liable to be dismissed. 4. After examining the record, this Court deems it proper to direct the competent authority of the respondents to appoint a teacher to the aforesaid school forthwith to prevent loss of education, as education is a fundamental right of every citizen.

Learned Additional A.G. has challenged the maintainability of the petition, stating that posting of teacher is an administrative matters made based on need and not on the request of an individual; therefore the petition is liable to be dismissed. 4. After examining the record, this Court deems it proper to direct the competent authority of the respondents to appoint a teacher to the aforesaid school forthwith to prevent loss of education, as education is a fundamental right of every citizen. In case of failure, appropriate action in terms of Article 204 of the Constitution shall be taken against the delinquent official. 5. This petition stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE JUDGE Karar_Hussain/PS*
